title: BLDP Geographic Market Performance and Regional Strategy
text: Ballard Power Systems serves diverse geographic markets with distinct dynamics. Europe emerged as strongest market with over 1,600 fuel cell buses in operation and 50%+ of H1 2021 revenue. European city bus adoption accelerated through 2024-2025 with platform wins from major OEMs including Solaris, New Flyer, and Wrightbus. UK and European hydrogen bus infrastructure development provides relatively mature market compared to other geographies. North America showed mixed results with strategic focus on New Flyer partnership (500-engine agreement). Canadian operations and CP Rail relationship support rail segment development. Asia-Pacific, particularly China, faced headwinds from policy uncertainty and underperformance of Weichai-Ballard joint venture. Strategic review of China operations underway with potential restructuring of joint venture participation. Emerging markets including Egypt (MCV bus order for EU deliveries) and other developing regions show longer-term potential as hydrogen infrastructure develops. Regional revenue distribution in 2024-2025 reflected European dominance with bus focus, North American rail/stationary growth potential, and reassessed approach to China given underperformance. Geographic diversification strategy prioritizes mature markets (Europe/North America) with developed bus OEM relationships while maintaining optionality in high-growth potential markets pending infrastructure development.
